sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy Cashien without the requirements

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Jolly good show on getting into the White Knights, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now how can I help you old [boy/gal/bean]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... So when can I start doing missions for the Temple Knights, and get some better Temple Knight armour and weapons and stuff?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, I may have a mission for you, but it seems you are not quite ready for it yet.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy Cashien to start the quest

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Jolly good show on getting into the White Knights, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now how can I help you young [fellow/mistress]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... So when can I start doing missions for the Temple Knights, and get some better Temple Knight armour and weapons and stuff?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, now that you are a White Knight we can process your application form correctly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how long will that take?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Already done, old bean! The scales of justice never sleep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, right.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: So then, dear gal; with that in mind, we have a mission for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Great! Am I to be flung to some far off and exotic land to defeat some evil genius?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: What? Ah, no actually. We're sending you to Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Hmm, what's happening in Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, you were about to tell me.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: No, no, no, nothing happening in Ardougne. However, there is something going on in Witchaven to the south-east of Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? I mean, you're not a little... senile, maybe?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Haw, Haw, Haw, very funny old bean. I need you to report to Col O'Niall in Witchaven. He's a retired Temple Knight, but his clearance is still active. He will be able to fill you in.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Remember to use the Temple Knight code phrase: 'In Falador, the geese fly backwards on Tuesdays'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, I'm still not sure this isn't some giant wind up.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Nonsense, my [man/gal], we take our work very seriously. The fate of the world rests in our hands, don't you know.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Hmm, you'll need to get your CommOrb before I send you off old bean.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Oh, before you go, I just need to update your CommOrb with the new mission parameters.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now, is there anything else I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I'm not interested.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: As you wish, old bean. Do let me know if you reconsider.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy Cashien after losing the commorb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Tiffy, I lost my CommOrb...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Free up some inventory space first, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Very careless of you, what? Here, take another, but be careful of this one!

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy Cashien after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Jolly good show on getting into the White Knights, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now how can I help you young [fellow/mistress]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Sir Tiffy.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Greetings, [player name]. Anything to report?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, sir, but I'm working on it.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Good show! That's the spirit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Savant! Good to speak to you again!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], I am so glad you've been assigned this mission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me too. So, do you have anything you can tell me?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Only what is already available through your CommOrb I'm afraid. I'm currently collating a lot of other data pertaining to Witchaven. I'll keep you updated if anything turns up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Savant. I know I can count on you.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Savant out.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Well hello there; welcome to our little village. Pray, stay awhile.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: The blessings of Saradomin be with you child.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me sir, would you be Col. O'Niall?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: I might. Who's asking?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name], sir. Sir Tiffy Cashien sent me.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm... In Falador the geese fly backwards on Tuesdays?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: What? Are you mad [boy/girl]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhh. I thought it was a Temple Knight, secret code phrase.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: I see Sir Tiffy still has his fun with the Initiates. Sigh, I asked for a Knight and I get a whelp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whelp? I am a full member of the Temple Knights, sir!

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Like I said, a whelp. And don't 'Sir' me [boy/girl], I work for a living.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right sir, I mean Colonel.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Jake will do fine [boy/girl].

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Now what do you want to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the mission?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Well, I started to notice something about three weeks ago.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: The Mayor called a meeting of the town council. Nothing too strange about that, in and of itself.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: He was acting a bit strange, like he'd taken a blow to the head or something. At the end of the meeting he announces that he's going to be renovating the shrine just west of the town.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Now, I thought that was a bit off since he's about as religious as a dead dog on a termite mound.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eeew!

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Quiet down, I'm talking here.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: I could have let that slip, save for the things he's been bringing in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, like surface to air ballistas, intercontinental exploding fireballs and quasi-futuristic henchman uniforms?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The essential tools for world domination?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: No, mining equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound very evil?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: As well as the mining equipment, he's been bringing in rune essence in large quantities.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Far beyond what's needed for a small village such as this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, that sounds suspicious. I'll look into it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Yes, some of the villagers have been disappearing overnight and reappearing the next day in a dazed and confused state.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure they're not just getting drunk?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: They could be I suppose, but I'd rather you made sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Anything else you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Witchaven.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Well, what's to say. This place is falling apart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I noticed it's a bit run down.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Run down? Hah! This place used to be a bustling hive of industry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to it?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: The Fishing Platform happened to it.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: When you introduce a large scale operation like the Fishing Platform, it takes away the business from the little guy.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Almost overnight Witchaven became a ghost town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's very sad. However, you can't stand in the way of progress.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: I'm going to pretend you didn't say that girl.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Remember, bigger is not always better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are the important people in Witchaven?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Hmm. There are only three people I recommend speaking to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, who would that be?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Firstly go speak to Mayor Hobb. He was the first to be acting strangely.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: As well as that, he isn't looking too healthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speak to the Mayor, check.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Next, speak to Brother Maledict at the church.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: He speaks to a lot of the villagers and has a good feel for what's going on in town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brother Maledict, right.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Finally, speak to Holgart the fisherman. I haven't seen him sailing in a while. That is definitely a bit off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Holgart, oh yes I've spoken to him before.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: That covers about everyone, but don't just limit yourself to those three. Others may have some insight into what's been happening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do Col. O'Niall. Thanks for the advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's enough for now.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Stay sharp [boy/girl].

sections.title: Talking to Col. O'Niall before getting all the information

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me Col. O'Niall. I need some more information.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: What do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Jeb
lines.text: You are not part of us. Leave us alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Holgart.

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: Well, hello again m'hearty. How're yer fare?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Busy as always. Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: 'Fraid not [lad/lass]. I've been washed up and left to dry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pardon?

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: Scuppered I am, keelhauled and bottomed out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: I'm destitute. I can sail no more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh my, that's terrible! What happened?

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: About two weeks ago, I was hauled up in front of the Mayor and he tells me I've broken his new 'Articles of sailing'.

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: He tells me that under his new laws my boat is forfeit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He took your boat?

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: Aye. The Mayor has given it to Jeb Wormwood. He is running the boat to the Fishing Platform now.

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: Speaking of which, since you were last there it's got a might bit stranger. You really should take a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do that Holgart. I hope you get your boat back soon.

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: Aye. Fare thee well.

lines.speaker: Holgart
lines.text: I don't know what to do without my boat.

sections.title: Talking to Jeb after Holgart says to ask him about a boat

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand you can take me to the Fishing Platform.

lines.speaker: Jeb
lines.text: Yes, we can do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you take me please?

lines.speaker: Jeb
lines.text: Board the boat and we shall depart.

sections.title: Talking to Bailey at the Fishing platform

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Bailey.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Well, hello again, [player name]. What brings you back out here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just looking around.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Are you insane? Have you seen what's happening out there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean the sickly looking lobsters?

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Yes the mutant lobsters! I swear at any minute they are going to break down the door and attack me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They seem fairly peaceful so far.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Well, true, but you can't be too careful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When did they start appearing?

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Not long after you rescued Kennith.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: They get hauled out of the sea as ordinary giant lobsters, the fishermen hit them with a mallet then a Sea Slug jumps into the crack.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: After a while they turn green, before jumping back into the sea and swimming off towards the mainland.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This sounds very serious. Hmm, I'd best get back to the mainland and see where these lobsters are heading.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Please be quick, I can't leave whilst those things are out there.

sections.title: Talking to Jeb at the Fishing platform

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Jeb.

lines.speaker: Jeb
lines.text: Business here is complete. Do you wish to return to the dry land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm going to stay a while.

lines.speaker: Jeb
lines.text: Then stay if that is your wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, let's go back.

lines.speaker: Jeb
lines.text: Then board the rowing boat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you, sir.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: And a good day to you my [son/daughter].

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: I am Brother Maledict, at your service.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name], at yours. How are you?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Well, to be perfectly honest with you, I am a little concerned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really, what's up?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: It's my congregation. They seem to be drifting away from me.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: With all the hardships this small village has endured in the past few years, the people of Witchaven have never needed the solace of Saradomin more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's understandable.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Yes indeed. However, less and less people have been attending my services lately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see why that would worry you. Do you have any idea why they would not attend?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: The only thing I can think of, is that the Mayor has asked them to help improve the shrine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that would make sense. Hmm, one more thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me again, what's worrying you?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: It's my congregation. They seem to be drifting away from me.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: With all the hardships this small village has endured in the past few years, the people of Witchaven have never needed the solace of Saradomin more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's understandable.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Yes indeed. However, less and less people have been attending my services lately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see why that would worry you. Do you have any idea why they would not attend?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: The only thing I can think of, is that the Mayor has asked them to help improve the shrine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that would make sense. Hmm, one more thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the shrine?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Ah yes, one of the wonders of Witchaven. That particular shrine has existed since before Witchaven was even founded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! Now that is old.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Over one hundred and fifty years old in fact.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: The role I now fulfill was originally given to one of the Temple Guardians, but in time that has been delegated to one of the ordained, such as myself.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: The shrine was abandoned when the village came into being. Since then it has fallen into disrepair and other less savoury elements have moved in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must be pleased that the Mayor is renovating the shrine.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Well yes, it came as something of a pleasant shock. I only hope he will let me visit it soon.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: He wants me to wait until it's ready to unveil.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the people of Witchaven.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Ah, the people of Witchaven are the salt of the earth. Hardworking and very generous. Well, usually that is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not anymore?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Oh, most of them certainly. It's just that something has changed over the past few weeks.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: It's nothing I can put my finger on. My flock aren't exactly unfriendly anymore, just different.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Well, they used to be very rigorous in attending my services. Now they seem almost apathetic.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: They wander around the village in a listless state, everyone except the Mayor that is.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Since the opening of the Fishing Platform he was a man beset by worries. The only thing that occupied his mind was how to rescue his village from financial collapse.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Ordinarily, I would welcome his new found drive and energy, especially as it is directed towards renovating the shrine

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: I'm not sure but something about him has me worried.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps he thinks the shrine could bring new interest to the village?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Perhaps. Maybe I'm being a little paranoid. I'll give it some thought.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: In the meantime, is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's enough for now.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Peace be with you child.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you, Brother Maledict.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: And a good day to you again, [player name]. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Well hello there; welcome to our little village. Pray, stay a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you. Would you be the Mayor?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Indeed. Mayor Eustace Hobb, in fact. Do you like our home?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's certainly very quiet.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Yes, quiet and undisturbed. Just how we like it.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Tell me, what brings you to Witchaven?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to relax.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: That's good, yes, that's good. Witchaven is a nice quiet village - very relaxing, good fishing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you alright? You seem a little, hazy.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: It's nothing, I'm just a little tired at the moment. So much work, so much to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really, what are you working on?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: To the West, the shrine needs... work, yes work. Things must be made better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, um. Yes. Indeed. I'm sure Saradomin would be pleased.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Saradomin... Why are you really here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like I said, I'm here to...

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Are you sure that's the only reason you are here, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm also here to visit a friend, Jake O'Niall.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Ahh, then this all makes sense. Yes, this is very good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, how did you know my name?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: You told me when you came in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no I didn't.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: It does not matter. One of my... villagers, must have mentioned that you are here.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Is there anything else you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Witchaven?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: The land has seen much sorrow, much loss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you talking about the Fishing Platform?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Fishing Platform? Oh yes, that. No. The platform will do much for this place.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: The platform heralds a new beginning for Witchaven, undreamed of by those who built it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought it took away all the trade?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: This is true, but it has brought us so much more in return, so much more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, and I thought the village hated the Fishing Platform.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: It was... a necessary step.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Is there anything else you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What improvements are you making to the shrine?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Ahh, the shrine - the future, our destiny, our heritage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: That which the shrine holds can unlock a new path for all the village of Witchaven. And in time the whole of Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, the whole of Gielinor? I thought it was just some old shrine to Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: The history of that shrine should not be overlooked. It is ancient beyond your knowledge and much that shaped our history is contained within its walls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a very interesting place. I may have to take a look.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: There is much to be done before it is opened.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it's not completed yet?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: When you are ready, it may be that you will be unable to keep yourself away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, you really are a strange person.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Is there anything else you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know you look very ill?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Ill? No, merely tired.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you've turned a slightly sickly colour.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: A touch of food poisoning perhaps.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: This has been known to turn the face and give the impression of severe illness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've seen food poisoning. People normally don't look like... that.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Time is all that is needed. Yes, time to... get better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I hope you get better soon.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Soon, all will be well again. Have no fear.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Is there anything else you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing at the moment thanks.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Then please feel free to stay in our village a while longer. Farewell.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Hello again, [player name].

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Is there anything else you need to know?

sections.title: Talking to Col. O'Niall after talking to Mayor Hobb

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: So [boy/girl], what have you learned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something is definitely very wrong here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mayor Hobb has confiscated Holgart's boat and given it to someone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As well as this, there's something very... fishy about him.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Hmm, fishy you say. Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Brother Maledict is worried that his congregation is being led astray.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Did he say anything more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The shrine to the west of the village has been consecrated to Saradomin for centuries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: However, last time he was there, there was a very definite unpleasant feeling in the air.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Sounds like this shrine needs a thorough investigation.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: I recommend taking a good look down there - see if anything is amiss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was thinking the same thing. I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Attempting to push the wall before talking about the shrine
Pushing the wall without the commorb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better carry my CommOrb so that Savant can see what's in there.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the open wall without the commorb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better carry my CommOrb so that Savant can see what's in there.

sections.title: Contacting commorb before scanning the shrine

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks Savant. I was just checking this thing still worked!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You shouldn't really waste my time like this, [player name]...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Still, no harm done I guess! Call me if you need me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay Savant, will do.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Savant out.

sections.title: Scanning the commorb in the wrong place
Scanning the commorb while in combat
Scanning the commorb near the door

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], hang on a second.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the problem?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: The markings on that door look familiar. Can you take a scan for me please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Savant needs to see this. I better get my Comm Orb.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I thought so. Those glyphs look similar to some ancient Saradomin runes I studied at the academy.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Hmm, I know a man who can translate them for us, just be careful as he's not a member of the Knights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... So how am I supposed to show him the glyphs?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Empty some space out of your pack and use the CommOrb to get a transcription of the glyphs.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I have added a transcript of the runes into your pack.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: The man you need to see is called Jorral. You can find him in his tower north-west of Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fantastic - I'll get right on it.

sections.title: Contacting the commorb after scanning the doors

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Savant?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Yes, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you think of those doors? Spooky hey?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Very, though I can't shake the feeling that I've seen something like that before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They did look really out of place. I wonder who put them there?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: The answer is probably contained within those strange glyphs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I only hope that Jorral can translate them.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: He has a very good track record in translation of ancient runes.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Although, to be fair he does tend to get a bit obsessive at times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the warning Savant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speak to you soon.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Savant out.

sections.title: Contacting commorb after losing door transcription

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Yes, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand that you are something of a historian?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Hah! I am the foremost historian in the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I was wondering if you could translate something for me please?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Possibly, I am very busy you see and my services don't come cheap.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, how much are we talking?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Well, I am very busy, but I could fit you in sometime next month.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was a bit longer than I was willing to wait. Can't you fit me in anytime sooner?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: I'm afraid not. You see, I have this ongoing series of texts that need to be catalogued as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Psst, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Savant?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Drat, I was hoping he wouldn't notice.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Excuse me, but are you talking to your backpack?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um, no. (whispers) Savant, help.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Uh. Tell him that you're working for a wizard who's sending you telepathic instructions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, err, am working for a wizard who is giving me telepathic instructions.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Riiiight. Okay. What does this err, voice, want you to do?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: We have a front company who's keeping Jorral on retainer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We have a...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No! Hang on, I wasn't finished.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Pardon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not you.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Can I finish now? Anyway, tell him you are working for Petmel Books.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently, I'm working for Petmel Books and this document is from them.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Oh, right! That explains the mild insanity. No problem; hand over the document and I'll have a look

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um, I don't appear to have it on me.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Well, run along and fetch it then.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Hmm. Interesting, very interesting.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: It's an early variation on a script used by the priests of Saradomin a long time ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does it say?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Look, you can see how the sentence structure and grammar evolved from the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, yes I'm sure it's very interesting but what does it say?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Hmph. It seems this text pertains to a great battle between the temple of Saradomin and something called the Mahey'ish Medron.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Mavis Merton?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Mahey'sh Medron. I think it's a corruption of an even earlier language and actually refers to something called Mother Mallum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Well, there isn't a whole lot more. They managed to trap the Mother Mallum behind a mystical prison where she was to wait for all time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thanks for that. You have been a great help.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Any time. I recommend a doctor for those voices, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, right. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], come in. This is Savant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fantastic, I'm here Savant.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: That was the final piece of the puzzle I needed.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I've cross-referenced the name Mahey'ish Medron with some other texts and been able to piece together some of the history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Finally, some solid information.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Indeed. It seems this isn't the first time the Temple Knights have run across this Mother Mallum character.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, why doesn't that surprise me?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Yes, it seems that centuries ago we discovered that a great evil had taken control of some of the most influential people in the world.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: That great evil was the Mother Mallum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, it took the few remaining uncorrupted knights all their wit and strength to devise a method of removing sea slug possession.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, that means we can free those poor people who have already fallen.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Once freed, the Temple Knights pursued a great crusade to destroy the Mother Mallum. Unfortunately, she was too powerful and the best they could do was to trap her inside a mystical prison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks like she's breaking free of that prison.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Right, the best thing we can do then is to repair or reenforce it before she escapes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did your research reveal how she was trapped in the first place?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No, I hit a brick wall after that. We had a dragon crash land on our headquarters a few years ago and the resulting fire destroyed a lot of our records.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drat, that complicates things.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I think the best thing you can do is take a look around Witchaven, speak to some of the locals and see if there are any clues to be found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Righty ho. I'll get to it. Thanks Savant.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Don't mention it, [player name].

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Savant out.

sections.title: Contacting the commorb after learning about Mother Mallum

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, Savant. Can you tell me about the Mother Mallum again please?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Sure thing, [player name].

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, this isn't the first time the Temple Knights have encountered the Mother Mallum.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Centuries ago we discovered that a great evil had taken control of some of the most influential people in the world.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: That great evil was the Mother Mallum.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It took the few remaining uncorrupted knights all their wit and strength to devise a method of removing sea slug possession.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Now the best thing we could to is to repair or reinforce it before she escapes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the reminder Savant.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Don't mention it, [player name].

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Savant out.

sections.title: Talking to Col. O'Niall after learning abotu Mother Mallum

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Col. O'Niall, this is worse than we thought.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: I doubt that, but go on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is something trapped under the town of Witchaven.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something the Temple Knights fought in ages past.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something ancient and very, very evil.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: That's an awful big something girl.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Care to be more specific?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's called the Mother Mallum and may have some connection to the sea slugs.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Hmm, it can't be a coincidence that the Mayor is 'renovating' the shrine. He must be preparing to break the Mother Mallum free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I agree, but what can we do about it?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Hmm, it might be worth speaking to that priest of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: See if he's got any more light to shed on this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! The temple of Saradomin has had a shrine here for centuries. I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's enough for now.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Stay sharp [boy/girl].

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Anything new to report?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but I was wondering if I could ask you some more questions?

sections.title: Talking to Maledict after learning about Mother Mallum

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Maledict.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, Maledict.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Hmm. Oh, yes. We are Maledict.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you feeling alright?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: It's nothing, just a little tired at the moment. So much work, so much to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds hauntingly familiar.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Was there something you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something about the Shrine to Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I have discovered a hidden passageway leading to a huge pair of double doors.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I thought you might know something about what is behind them.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Ahh. Right. No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean, no?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Most of the history of the shrine is contained within the holy book of Saradomin. We have yet to read that portion of the text.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Can I have a look please?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again with this no business. Why not?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Because the three pages pertaining to the shrine's history have been stolen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, right, sorry. Would you like me to find them for you?

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: It is imperative that you find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: Peace be with you.

lines.speaker: Brother Maledict
lines.text: The joys of family are not to be underestimated child.

sections.title: Talking to Ezekial Lovecraft after Brother Maledict asks about the pages

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. How are things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Frustrating, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Sorry to hear that.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Is there any way I can help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm not sure. Brother Maledict has asked me to find some pages that were torn out of his holy book.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: What, like this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, just like that, but I can't seem to find...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where on Gielinor did you get that?

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Someone pushed it under my door just before you arrived. I went outside to see who it was but they'd already gone by the time I got there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Strange. Do you mind if I take that?

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Sure, but I don't think you have enough space in your pack to hold it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on, I'll just make some room.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Sure, here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Glad I could be of service. Anything else you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, you sound just like the mayor?

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Really? That's the nicest thing anyone has ever said.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: I admire him a great deal. He works so hard for this town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You look like him as well?

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Hmm, I don't think I do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No really, what with the unhealthy skin tone and bulging eyes. The resemblance is uncanny.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Well, I haven't spoken to him in a while. I don't get out much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see why.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Maybe the Mayor has finally seen how much I admire him and has grown to admire me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything's possible I suppose. Wait a minute, you mean you've always looked that odd?

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Hey, I resent that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, it's just... Nevermind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You are a bit too strange for me. Bye.

lines.speaker: Ezekial Lovecraft
lines.text: Sniff. Yes, everyone says that.

sections.title: Talking to Col. O'Niall while looking for the final page

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: [player name], there you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jake, I hope you can help me out here.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Perhaps. What is it that you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I... Hang on a moment, you're looking a bit sickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no. They got to you too, didn't they?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Are you sure this is not some elaborate deception on my part, to fool them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't know, but how can I trust you?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Because you seek the last page of Maledict's holy book. It is right here for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That seems a tad convenient.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Perhaps, but at the end of the day it is what you need. Do you want it or not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not so sure that this is a good idea, but yes, I'll take it.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Well, you'll need to free up space for three items in your backpack first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll do that now.

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: It has been torn into three pieces. You will need to stick them back together before you can use them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, another complication I didn't need. I wonder if swamp paste will do the trick?

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Don't mention it.

sections.title: Using swamp paste on the pages

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], stop!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? I'm only trying to glue these pages back together.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Please don't use oil-rich swamp paste on an ancient parchment. It'll ruin it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, how do I stick the pages back together?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, lucky for you, my preliminary sea slug analysis has just finished.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It shows that if you render the slug down, it will form a very sticky glue that will dry clear.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You will need to find someone who has years of experience in aquatic cooking or it could go horribly wrong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem Savant. I'll snap to it right away.

sections.title: Talking to Bailey after learning about the glue

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Waaaaah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrrgh!

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Oh, sorry, [player name]. I thought you were one of those fishermen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: N-No problem. It was just a surprise that's all.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Well, I am scared half to death out here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have something that could help you out.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Really, great!

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have a page that should give me the instructions on how to eliminate what could be the source of the sea slugs.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Then why haven't you used it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's been torn up into three pieces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it on good authority that you may be able to help me out by rendering a dead sea slug down for glue.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Well, you are in luck. I had been cooking a new batch of gumbo for my dinner. However, I've let it cook too long and it's become very thick and sticky.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: It should make the perfect base for your glue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! That was good timing wasn't it.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Uncanny really.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Do you have a dead sea slug I can use?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah. Um. No, not at the moment.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Well, get back to me when you have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here you go.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Right, I'll get cooking.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Right, there you go.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: That should do the trick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks very much. I'll get right to work on the parchment.

lines.speaker: Bailey
lines.text: Good luck.

sections.title: Talking to Col. O'Niall after losing the commorb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um Colonel, I lost my CommOrb...

lines.speaker: Col. O'Niall
lines.text: Very careless of you, what? Here, take another, but be careful with this one!

sections.title: Opening the door without the commorb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better carry my CommOrb so that Savant can see what's in there.

sections.title: Opening the door with the runes and commorb

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Ah, so good of you to finally join us [player name]. To be honest, I never thought you'd make it this far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Mayor Hobb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Oh come now [player name]. Surely you are smart enough to know when you've been played for a fool?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahh, the penny drops. You got me to do your dirty work for you.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Indeed. The enchantment placed upon this door by the Temple Knights prevented anyone not working in their interests from opening it.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: With your superb timing, our plans have been advanced quicker than even we could have anticipated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what are they? What are your plans?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: You'd really like to know? Well, it seems the least I can do for you after all your hard work.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Behold, the beauteous visage of our beloved Prince.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this is the Mother Mallum?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Mwahahaha... Fool. No, this is not our Great Mother, though I sincerely wish you had the chance to gaze upon her.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Sadly for you, this will be the last thing you ever see.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Behold, the beauteous visage of our beloved Prince.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this is the Mother Mallum?

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Mwahahaha... Fool. No, this is not our Great Mother, though I sincerely wish you had the chance to gaze upon her.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: No! Do you realise what you have done?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I have destroyed one of the great evils infesting this world.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: You fool! Did you think it would be so easy? The Prince will be reborn in all his glory!

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: Now you face the true wrath of the Mother Mallum!

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: Calm yourself, my child. We anticipated this eventuality. Go now and prepare the throne for our arrival.

lines.speaker: Mayor Hobb
lines.text: By your command, my queen.

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: So then, [player name], it is just us. What shall we do with you?

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: Your mind is like an open book to us. Your very essence laid bare before our sight.

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: You have great potential, [player name]. We should destroy you now, lest you interfere with our plans further.

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: Or we could take you whole and make you an extension of our will.

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: After millennia, this husk is almost spent and requires replacement. Yes, you will make an excellent host.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never! I'll never serve you. I shall fight until my last breath before giving in.

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: Yes, that's it, fight, fight with all your heart.

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: It's always so much sweeter when they resist us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nooooooooo!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], are you there? We've been having a few technical problems...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Savant! Help me, please, for the love of Saradomin!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: By the gods! I'll get you out of there immediately! Initiating emergency teleport.

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: Come with us, [player name]. Come and be with us for all eternity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Savant!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: She's blocking my magic! One second, I'll try something else.

lines.speaker: Mother Mallum
lines.text: Ahh, [player name], it is useless to resist. Now we shall become as one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Savant, now would be a good time!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Right, I think I've got it this time. Just one more second.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], are you there?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], are you okay? Speak to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew! Yes, Savant. That was too close for comfort.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Thank Saradomin!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I thought we'd lost you there for a moment.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I think you'd better talk to Sir Tiffy about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed. I'll just get my breath back first.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy Cashien after killing the slug prince

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Free up some inventory space first, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Jolly good show on getting into the White Knights, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now how can I help you young [fellow/mistress]?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Greetings, [player name]. Anything to report?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sir Tiffy, it's all gone horribly wrong!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Nonsense, dear gal, it can't be as bad as all that.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Why don't you relax a little and tell Sir Tiffy what's wrong, hmm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The town of Witchaven has been overrun by mind controlling sea slugs!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Hmm, go on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the Mayor of the town was first, then brother Maledict, and finally Colonel O'Niall.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Oh dear, that is a shame. Please go on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, they tricked me into opening the prison that kept their Queen imprisoned.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ahh, the Mother Mallum. Dear-oh-dear, this isn't good news at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it isn't all bad. I did manage to kill the Prince.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Excellent! That should delay her plans somewhat. If she follows the same pattern as last time, the important figures of Gielinor will be her next targets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That cannot be allowed to happen! What can we do about it?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I think we are going to have to increase our surveillance somewhat.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Seeing as you already have a familiarity with the sea slugs, I am going to place you personally in charge of this mission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you think I'm ready for it, Sir Tiffy.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Of course you are, my gal. Now you will need access to some of the more restricted resources of the Temple Knights.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: So, with immediate effect, I am promoting you within the Temple Knights. Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I only wish it were under better circumstances.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! I am honoured.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Tish, pashaw and nonsense. It's about time you were rewarded for your work.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now, with this promotion comes some new responsibilities. In due time you will have access to the Temple Knight headquarters, but for now we need to collate our

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: information and plan ahead.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Report back to me in a little while and I'll have some new tasks for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes sir, I'll keep my eyes open for any signs of sea slug infiltration.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Good lass. Oh and Colonel O'Niall has been placed on our disavowed list. He should be considered extremely dangerous and is to be killed on sight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, no problem. Well, I'll see you soon.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ta ta, old bean.
